name: pitfalls-websocket
description: WebSocket server and client patterns with heartbeat and reconnection. Use when implementing real-time features, debugging connection issues, or reviewing WebSocket code. Triggers on: WebSocket, wss, heartbeat, reconnect, real-time.
capabilities: Review WebSocket server implementation for common pitfalls
Add ping-pong heartbeat pattern to detect dead connections
Implement client reconnection with exponential backoff
Validate WebSocket message formats and types
Debug connection drops and reconnection issues
author: BarisSozen
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only TypeScript code examples for educational purposes. No executable code, file system access, network calls, or command execution capabilities. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting code examples in markdown documentation as executable patterns. The previous audit (skill-report.json) correctly identified this as safe.
valueStatement: WebSocket implementations often fail silently due to missing heartbeat or reconnection logic. This skill provides verified patterns for robust real-time connections that survive network issues and handle message validation correctly.
userTitle: Implement WebSocket servers with best practices
limitations: Does not write code for you - provides patterns and review guidance
Does not test running WebSocket servers
Does not configure deployment environments or load balancers

useCases.title: Build production WebSocket server
useCases.description: Create WebSocket server with heartbeat, proper message handling, and connection lifecycle management

useCases.title: Implement resilient client
useCases.description: Build WebSocket client that reconnects automatically after network interruptions

useCases.title: Audit real-time code
useCases.description: Review WebSocket implementation for missing heartbeat, error handling, or reconnection logic

promptTemplates.title: Check Server Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my WebSocket server code. Check if it shares the HTTP port, has heartbeat implemented, and handles client connections correctly.

promptTemplates.title: Add Heartbeat
promptTemplates.scenario: Add ping-pong to detect dead connections
promptTemplates.prompt: Add heartbeat ping-pong mechanism to my WebSocket server. Show how to detect dead connections and terminate them gracefully.

promptTemplates.title: Client Reconnect
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement WebSocket client reconnection with exponential backoff. Include connection state tracking and error logging.

promptTemplates.title: Message Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my WebSocket message handling. Add proper JSON parsing, type validation, and error responses for invalid messages.

bestPractices: Use same port for WebSocket server and HTTP server to simplify deployment
Implement heartbeat every 30 seconds to detect stale connections early
Reset reconnection attempt counter on successful connection
Validate and type all incoming messages before processing
antiPatterns: Skipping heartbeat - leads to ghost connections consuming resources
No reconnection logic - clients stay disconnected after network issues
Processing raw messages without validation - security risk

faq.question: What tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Uses natural language understanding for code review.

faq.question: What are the connection timeout limits?
faq.answer: Heartbeat interval defaults to 30 seconds. Maximum reconnection delay is capped at 30 seconds.

faq.question: Can I integrate with existing WebSocket libraries?
faq.answer: Patterns work with ws, Socket.io, and native WebSocket. Concepts apply to any WebSocket implementation.

faq.question: Is my WebSocket data stored or logged?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reviews code you share. No data is stored or transmitted.

faq.question: Why do connections drop unexpectedly?
faq.answer: Common causes: missing heartbeat causing timeout, load balancer idle timeout, network interruption, or server restart.

faq.question: How does this compare to Socket.io?
faq.answer: Socket.io provides built-in reconnection. This skill helps when building custom WebSocket solutions or migrating from Socket.io.
